subject: Ecology
title: Defining Wetlands
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Defining Wetlands.

hook.text: Wetlands are neither fully land nor fully water—they exist in the zone between.

buildup.text: A wetland is any area where water saturates the soil long enough to shape the ecosystem.

discovery.text: Marshes, swamps, bogs, fens, and floodplains are all types of wetlands.

twist.text: Some wetlands are wet only seasonally—dry for months, then flooded, yet still ecologically wetland.

climax.text: Wetlands cover just 6% of Earth's surface but provide services worth trillions of dollars.

punchline.text: Half water, half land, fully essential.

quiz.question: What defines a wetland?
options: Water saturates the soil long enough to shape the ecosystem
Any area with standing water year-round
